reasoning: I am voting for Biswajit Das and Mamata's government because my family's safety depends on the promise she made — no NRC in Bengal. When CAA became law in 2019, I understood that the BJP sees Muslims like us as a separate category of citizen, someone who must prove something. Mamata said no, and she has kept that word. My ration card works, my children go to school, Swasthya Sathi was there when I needed it. Shantanu Thakur may be our local candidate and a minister, but he belongs to the party that made CAA — the party that does not trust my family. I cannot vote for the party that looks at me differently than it looks at Hindus.
primary_drivers: Mamata's promise of no NRC in Bengal — existential security for my family
Fear that BJP's CAA makes Muslim citizenship precarious and separates us legally from Hindus
Dependence on AITC welfare programs that actually work (ration card, Swasthya Sathi, school access)
